rationale: My round-1 finding is ADDRESSED. populate.go:75 now resolves via workspace.DomainsDir(root) (flat-first three-tier precedence, verified in workspace.go:546-556→resolveArtifact); the doc comment (61-73) is corrected and the stale .mindspec/docs/domains comment is gone. TestDomainsNeedingPopulate_FlatTree (populate_test.go:174-190) writes ONLY to flat .mindspec/domains/ via writeFlatDomain and asserts [absent,stub]; on the old hardcoded .mindspec/docs/domains code that tree is absent → ReadDir IsNotExist → returns nil → got!=want → FAIL, so it genuinely proves flat enumeration. The canonical-tier test still pins .mindspec/docs/domains, so both tiers are covered. INDEPENDENT re-scan of every live read/enumerate op (ReadDir/Glob/Walk/ReadFile) over docs/{specs,adr,domains,core} across internal+cmd: ALL route through workspace.{Specs,Domains,Spec,Domain,ADR}Dir or the tier-aware docsRootRel (doctor/docs.go:52,164-170) — orphaned_beads, doctor/ownership, doctor/docs, instruct, adr show/parse/create, spec/list, domain/show/list, docsync:429. No flat-blind read remains; author's claim holds. Remaining .mindspec/docs joins are all carve-outs: display-only strings (docsync 516/580, manifestCheckName:85 — actual enum at ownership.go:69 uses DomainsDir), permanent multi-prefix matchers (domainManifestRelPaths/domainsTreeRoots), canonical-tier fallback (SpecDir:416), migration source (mover/rewrite/migrate). Fix-3 skips justified. build/vet clean; ownership/doctor/validate suites PASS.

findings.issue: DomainsNeedingPopulate now enumerates via workspace.DomainsDir(root) (populate.go:75), and TestDomainsNeedingPopulate_FlatTree (populate_test.go:174-190) writes a flat-only .mindspec/domains/ tree and asserts the absent+stub domains are returned — a test that returns nil (fails) on the old hardcoded .mindspec/docs/domains path, so it truly pins flat enumeration. The canonical-tier sibling test is retained, covering both tiers. Doc comment corrected.

Re-scanned every read/enumerate site across internal+cmd. All resolve through flat-aware accessors: doctor/orphaned_beads.go:49 SpecsDir, doctor/ownership.go:69 DomainsDir, doctor/migration.go:47 SpecsDir, doctor/docs.go:52 (docsRel from docsRootRel→Dir(SpecsDir), tier-aware) + :185 SpecsDir, instruct.go:105/257 SpecDir + :293 SpecsDir, adr/show.go:33 + parse.go:183/262 + create.go:198 ADRDir, spec/list.go:22 SpecsDir, domain/show.go:32 DomainDir + :74 SpecsDir, domain/list.go:101 DomainsDir, docsync.go:429 DomainsDir. No remaining live flat-blind read found; author's 'none remain' claim VERIFIED.

manifestCheckName (doctor/ownership.go:85) returns docs/domains/<d>/OWNERSHIP.yaml purely as a Check.Name LABEL — the real enumeration (canonicalDomains:68-82) uses workspace.DomainsDir; pinned by exact-string tests, no .mindspec/ prefix (AC17-invisible), joins nothing on disk. docsync.go:516/580 embed .mindspec/docs/domains inside fmt.Sprintf error-hint text for r.AddError('internal-docs',...); the message also names the actual manifest path, and the lane's real read is docsync.go:429 DomainsDir. Both are display-only and reasonably skipped; cosmetic path drift on flat trees only, follow-up-worthy not blocking.

build + go vet clean; go test ./internal/ownership/... ./internal/doctor/... ./internal/validate/... all PASS. The two pre-existing instruct/harness failures (confirmed layout-independent in round 1) are untouched by this fix. Round-1 cleanup is undisturbed. No new issue.
